From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

1976 Italian crime comedy film

| The Cop in Blue Jeans | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Directed by | [Bruno Corbucci](/wiki/Bruno_Corbucci "Bruno Corbucci") |
| Screenplay by | * [Mario Amendola](/wiki/Mario_Amendola "Mario Amendola") * Bruno Corbucci[[1]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2013167-1) |
| Story by | * Mario Amendola * Bruno Corbucci[[1]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2013167-1) |
| Produced by | Galliano Juso[[2]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2013168-2) |
| Starring | * [Tomas Milian](/wiki/Tomas_Milian "Tomas Milian") * [Jack Palance](/wiki/Jack_Palance "Jack Palance") * [Maria Rosaria Omaggio](/wiki/Maria_Rosaria_Omaggio "Maria Rosaria Omaggio") |
| Cinematography | Nino Celeste[[1]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2013167-1) |
| Edited by | Daniele Alabiso[[1]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2013167-1) |
| Music by | [Guido & Maurizio De Angelis](/wiki/Guido_%26_Maurizio_De_Angelis "Guido & Maurizio De Angelis")[[1]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2013167-1) |
| Production company | Cinemaster[[2]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2013168-2) |
| Distributed by | Regional |
| Release date | * 11 March 1976 (1976-03-11) (Italy) |
| Running time | 95 minutes[[1]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2013167-1) |
| Country | Italy[[2]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2013168-2) |

***The Cop in Blue Jeans*** ([Italian](/wiki/Italian_language "Italian language"): *Squadra antiscippo*, [lit.](/wiki/Literal_translation "Literal translation") 'Anti-theft squad') is a 1976 Italian crime and comedy film directed by [Bruno Corbucci](/wiki/Bruno_Corbucci "Bruno Corbucci"). The film was a major commercial success and generated a film series consisting of eleven entries starring [Tomas Milian](/wiki/Tomas_Milian "Tomas Milian") as Inspector Nico Giraldi.

## Plot

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Cop_in_Blue_Jeans&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Plot")]

An undercover cop named Giraldi is assigned to the anti-mugging squad. He tries to capture one of the main [Rome](/wiki/Rome "Rome") thieves Achille Bertinari called Baron. But when the later robbed the American crime boss, Richard Russo, also known as Shelley, Giraldi understands that it is not an easy task to arrest Russo-Shelley, so he should resort to ruses to bring him down.

## Cast

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Cop_in_Blue_Jeans&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Cast")]

* [Tomas Milian](/wiki/Tomas_Milian "Tomas Milian") as Nico Giraldi
* [Jack Palance](/wiki/Jack_Palance "Jack Palance") as Norman Shelley / Richard J. Russo
* [Maria Rosaria Omaggio](/wiki/Maria_Rosaria_Omaggio "Maria Rosaria Omaggio") as Mrs. Cattani
* [Guido Mannari](/wiki/Guido_Mannari "Guido Mannari") as Achille Bertinari "Er Baronetto"
* Jack La Cayenne as Colombo
* Raf Luca as Gargiulo
* [Toni Ucci](/wiki/Toni_Ucci "Toni Ucci") as Lando Rossi "Grottaferrata"
* [Vincenzo Crocitti](/wiki/Vincenzo_Crocitti "Vincenzo Crocitti") as Er Zagaja
* [Benito Stefanelli](/wiki/Benito_Stefanelli "Benito Stefanelli") as Shelley's Henchman
* John P. Dulaney as Ballarin

## Production

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Cop_in_Blue_Jeans&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Production")]

*The Cop in Blue Jeans* was the first film where [Tomas Milian](/wiki/Tomas_Milian "Tomas Milian") would portray Marshall Nico Giraldi, nicknamed *Il Pirata* (transl. The Pirate).[[2]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2013168-2) The character would go on to be portrayed in 11 films between 1976 and 1984.[[2]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2013168-2) The producer Galliano Juso recalled that the idea for the character came when Bruno Corbucci and he were making a film titled *[Il trafficone](/wiki/Il_trafficone "Il trafficone")*.[[2]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2013168-2) While filming,
Juso had his purse stolen by thieves on Kawasaki motorcycles.[[2]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2013168-2) This even led to Corbucci and him to develop a film about an "anti-snatch" squad.[[2]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2013168-2)

The sequels to the film, *[Assassinio sul Tevere](/wiki/Assassinio_sul_Tevere "Assassinio sul Tevere")*, *[Delitto a Porta Romana](/wiki/Delitto_a_Porta_Romana "Delitto a Porta Romana")*, *[Crime at the Chinese Restaurant](/wiki/Crime_at_the_Chinese_Restaurant "Crime at the Chinese Restaurant")*, *[Delitto sull'autostrada](/wiki/Delitto_sull%27autostrada "Delitto sull'autostrada")*, *[Crime in Formula One](/wiki/Crime_in_Formula_One "Crime in Formula One")* and *[Cop in Drag](/wiki/Cop_in_Drag "Cop in Drag")* grew more comedic with each film.[[3]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2013169-3)[[4]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2013170-4)

## Release

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Cop_in_Blue_Jeans&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Release")]

*The Cop in Blue Jeans* was distributed theatrically in Italy on 11 March 1976.[[2]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2013168-2) It grossed a total of 2,013,807,160 [Italian lire](/wiki/Italian_lire "Italian lire").[[2]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2013168-2) Italian film historian and critic Roberto Curti stated that it was among the most profitable domestic productions of the year.[[3]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2013169-3) The film was released on DVD by Raro Video in Italy.[[2]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2013168-2)

## Reception

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Cop_in_Blue_Jeans&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Reception")]

From contemporary reviews, Scott Meek of the *[Monthly Film Bulletin](/wiki/Monthly_Film_Bulletin "Monthly Film Bulletin")* reviewed a 75-minute edition of the film. [[5]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEMeek1978225-5) Meek stated that the film had a fractured plot which "maybe largely due to the twenty minutes missing from this version, but it is doubtful if, even in its complete form the story would have been at all coherent" stating the film "is aimed, to an almost fetishistic degree, at the lowest common denominator of the young, male, Italian audience," and that film was far too full of "sexism, anti-gay jokes and dubbed American dialogue, mostly consisting of unimaginative terms of abuse."[[6]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEMeek1978226-6)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Cop_in_Blue_Jeans&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: See also")]
